verdicts.title: Stiffness-Calibrated LAD Compartmentalization as Genomic Selectivity Filter for TEAD Enhancer Access
verdicts.key_strength: Three-tier LAD selectivity model (cLAD/fLAD/non-LAD) is genuinely novel after exhaustive web search. CRISPR tethering causal test. Properly constructed null model (OR >= 4.0).
verdicts.key_concern: fLAD stiffness-modulation is parametric. Pigeot 2025 bioRxiv is convergent but does not pre-empt.
verdicts.novelty_evidence: Cosgrove 2025 (mechanoenhancers) uses ATAC-seq, no LAD stratification. Pigeot 2025 connects lamin+PRC2 at gene level, not enhancer selectivity. PubMed 'nuclear mechanics AND H3K27ac' = 4 papers. No paper proposes three-tier LAD selectivity filter.

post_cutoff_papers_found.relevance: Identifies mechanoenhancers but does NOT address LAD compartmentalization
ECM stiffness + lamin + PRC2 at gene level, not enhancer selectivity level

verdicts.title: Sequential Two-Phase Bivalent Enhancer Resolution: KDM6B Demethylation as Rate-Limiting Gate
verdicts.fix_required: Remove or replace unverifiable 'Whitworth 2024' citation with verified EP300-YAP reference (e.g., PMID 38048728)

citation_concerns.issue: Cannot be found via multiple web searches. Claim it supports (EP300 at YAP-TEAD) is independently confirmed, so this is a citation quality issue, not a hallucination of a supporting fact.

verdicts.key_strength: Two-phase temporal model with biochemically forced sequential ordering. KDM6B at enhancers strengthened by Zhu 2021 neuroblastoma precedent. Three-armed paralog experiment.
verdicts.key_concern: KDM6B vs KDM6A at enhancers under ECM stiffness is the central untested novel claim. Whitworth 2024 unverifiable.
verdicts.novelty_evidence: No paper proposes two-phase temporal model for bivalent enhancer resolution under ECM stiffness. KDM6B 2025 (Yu) showed promoter-level activity only. Zhu 2021 shows KDM6B at enhancers in neuroblastoma (supports but doesn't pre-empt ECM context).

verdicts.title: Kinetically Gated Epigenetic Memory at Mechanosensitive Super-Enhancers via BRD4-Scaffolded EP300 Retention
verdicts.fix_required: Revise H3K27ac base half-life from '30-90 min' to ~1.5h (in vivo enhancer measurement). Propagate through kinetic model to produce revised memory window estimate.

quantitative_concerns.claim: H3K27ac half-life 30-90 min at single nucleosomes
quantitative_concerns.impact: Memory window may be slightly longer or the effective ratio k_write/k_erase may be marginally different. Does not invalidate the model.

verdicts.key_strength: Kinetic rate model converts qualitative 'memory' into quantitative prediction. dBET6/JQ1 differential is an elegant mechanistic discriminator. Triptolide experiment cleanly dissects epigenetic vs. transcriptional memory.
verdicts.key_concern: High parameter sensitivity (4 PARAMETRIC rate constants). BRD4-CTD scaffold mechanism inferred from co-IP/STRING, not structurally characterized. 6-18h range is a 3x spread.
verdicts.novelty_evidence: No paper proposes BRD4-scaffolded EP300 retention as mechanism for SE memory post-softening. Mechanical memory exists (Yang 2014) but at gene expression level. SE-level H3K27ac memory with kinetic model is novel.

meta_validation.strongest_rationale: Most novel (three-tier LAD selectivity model), best-grounded (8/10), cleanest causal test (CRISPR tethering), all citations verified
meta_validation.calibration_assessment: Marginally permissive — H5-v2 at boundary with 4 PARAMETRIC claims. Overall appropriate.
meta_validation.recommendation: Proceed to session_analysis. All 3 hypotheses meet quality standards. H4-v2 is flagship.
